title: staleness extend playbooks scripts pack nodes
purpose: E5.2 defines staleness extend playbooks scripts pack nodes for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness.
scope: Owns `E5.2` only; siblings under `E5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §7 — Branch E — Knowledge & composition plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#7-branch-e-knowledge-composition-plane).
behavior_steps: When platform promotion lands playbooks, scripts, or pack fragments, reconcile-stale registers each reusable path in docs/manifest/staleness.json so downstream task cards and compose hits can mark dependencies stale.

On script or playbook change during pursuit, the staleness graph propagates to dependent task cards and [E2.2](E2.2-compose-query-catalog-list-components.md) compose results—workers reconcile before advancing implement steps.

Regeneration of [E1.1](E1.1-catalog-scripts-manifest-generated.md) and [E1.7](E1.7-catalog-platform-catalog-md-umbrella.md) reconciles graph entries with live catalog INDEX rows after platform drain completes.
Pack verify suites from [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) may assert graph completeness at goal_verify—missing nodes fail closed rather than allowing pursuit on orphan artifact paths.

If a promoted artifact lacks a staleness node or graph drift leaves dependents unmarked, pursuit stops at H2 until reconcile-stale or [D4.5](D4.5-platform-work-catalog-regeneration.md) restores traceability.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `E5.2`.

narrative: E5.2 defines staleness extend playbooks scripts pack nodes for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness. In practice, the system when platform promotion lands playbooks, scripts, or pack fragments, reconcile-stale registers each reusable path in docs/manifest/staleness.json so downstream task cards and compose hits can mark dependencies stale, then on script or playbook change during pursuit, the staleness graph propagates to dependent task cards and [e2.2](e2.2-compose-query-catalog-list-components.md) compose results—workers reconcile before advancing implement steps.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-e 2026-06-28 -->

The staleness graph extends beyond design docs to playbooks, scripts catalog nodes, and template-pack fragments—any reusable component that downstream pursuit depends on. When a script or playbook changes, dependent task cards and compose hits mark stale.

Extension connects Plane E catalog maturity to traceability: promoted platform artifacts are first-class staleness nodes, not orphan files. Regeneration of [E1.1](E1.1-catalog-scripts-manifest-generated.md) and [E1.7](E1.7-catalog-platform-catalog-md-umbrella.md) should reconcile graph entries. Pack verify suites ([F1.8](../full-automation/MASTER-F-branch-f---organization-plane.md)) may assert graph completeness.
